notes: Early HST Halpha epoch; no significant impact on old ejecta reported at this stage.
Bulk radio-emitting material interpreted as very cold or slowly expanding for roughly the first 50 days.
Still before the main old-ejecta flash-ionization signature emphasized later.
Second ejection phase inferred roughly two months after outburst.
Rapid rise of super-soft and hard X-rays after about day 115 implies ejecta mass of order at least 1e-5 Msun.
Hydrogen north and south of the star photoionized by this epoch.
Public Hubble image epoch. Ring radius is an approximate representative structural scale from literature, not a per-image measured radius.
Previously photoionized hydrogen had recombined over the following 51 days.
Material to the northwest and southeast had recombined by this stage.
Late Halpha epoch with most transient old-ejecta emission gone.
Historical shell-scale mass estimate; not synchronized to 2011 outburst epochs.
